nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hiliary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran.
English (of Norman origin):

from the Middle English and Old French male or female personal name Hilari(e), Ilari(e), and Elari(e), from Latin Hilarius and Hilaria ‘joyful’ (from Greek hilaros). The Latin name was chosen by many early Christians to express their joy and hope of salvation, and was borne by several saints, including a 4th-century bishop of Poitiers (died 368) noted for his vigorous resistance to the Arian heresy, and a 5th-century bishop of Arles. Largely due to veneration of Saint Hilarius of Poitiers, the name became popular in France in the forms Hilari and Hilaire, and was brought to England by the Norman conquerors. Although quite popular in France, it was only occasionally used in England, where it was confused with the name in 2 below.

variants.name: Ellery
meanings: variant of   Hillary  .
habitational name from Elleray in Windermere (Westmorland), probably from Old Norse elri ‘alder’ + vrá ‘nook’.
English:

variants.name: Gloor
meanings: Swiss German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Hilarius (see Hillary ).

variants.name: Hillier
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Surrey): occupational name for a roofer from Middle English hil(l)ier, hul(li)er, hel(li)er ‘roofer, slater, tiler’, agent nouns from the Middle English verb hil(i)en, hel(i)an, hul(i)en ‘to put a roof on; cover with a roof’.
French: from the personal name Hillier, a vernacular form of Hilaire (see Hillary ).

variants.name: Alario
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Orazio, Carlo, Rocco, Salvatore, Vita, Vitina.
Italian: from a vernacular form of the personal name Ilario, from Latin Hilarius (see Hillary ).
Italian (Sicily): probably a status name from dialect alariu ‘herald’.

variants.name: Glor
meanings: German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Hilarius (see Hillary ).

variants.name: Hillery
meanings: English: variant of   Hillary  . This name has long been established in Ireland (Clare).

variants.name: Hilaire
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Pierre, Antoine, Chantal, Edeline, Georges, Luc, Maryse, Mimose, Serge, Yolaine.
French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the personal name Hilaire, French form of Hillary 1. This surname is most common in Haiti. Compare Helaire .

variants.name: Hilleary
meanings: English (Essex): variant of   Hillary  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Allaire
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Andre, Lucien, Alphonse, Antoine, Armand, Emile, Michel, Pierre, Raoul, Raymonde, Rolande.
French: from the Old French personal name Allaire, a variant of Hilaire (see Hillary ). Compare Dallaire .

History: Jean and Charles Allaire, two brothers from the Poitou region of France, are recorded in Quebec City, QC, in 1662 and 1663, respectively, the years of their marriage. The descendants of one or another brother also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Dallaire, Delair, and Delaire. — Alexandre Allaire, a Huguenot refugee, brought the name from La Rochelle in France to New Rochelle, NY, in 1686, via the West Indies. He is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Hilario
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Julio, Alfredo, Luis, Abelardo, Arturo, Eduardo, Francisco, Juan, Mario, Pedro, Carlos, Cesar.  Portuguese Afonso, Joao.
Portuguese (Hilário) and Spanish: from a personal name ofthe same origin as Hillary 1.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Old German: Mighty battle maid
